title: "2nd screen."
date: 2008-06-30

### Post by ThomasWii on 2008-06-30
Hi, i have a LCD laptop and another screen, i want to make it so my desktop extends into this. I'm running 8.04 and System > Preferences > Screen Res, dose not work and the commands nvidia-settings dose not work, here is my xorg and i think that will be the easy way to set up on my PC.```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"vesa"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-40
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
```

Thanks
Thomas.

### Post by blazercist on 2008-06-30
yea nvidia-settings will not work if u arent running the nvidia driver, you need to find out which video card you have and install the correct driver.


if you dont kno what card you have you can post the output of 
```

lspci

```

and we will try to help

---

Doubt you will get dual monitor support in linux with that gpu :(

You could try setting it up using the command:

```
gksu displayconfig-gtk
```

;)

### Post by blazercist on 2008-06-30
yea that looks like an onboard GPU, if you have some spare cash get a nvidia card, nothing crazy.  nvidia has the best driver support IMO for linux and its just plain simple as pie to setup dual monitors with an nvidia card.

I'm really not familiar with that GPU but just because it works in windows doesn't mean it will work in linux automatically.  I don't know if there are any drivers for that card in linux.

### Post by blazercist on 2008-06-30
oh, its a laptop, I forgot you mentioned that in the original post.


I guess you are out of luck on the dual screens front.  why dont you do a quick google for "ubuntu dual monitor UniChrome Pro IGP (rev 01)"

edit: after taking my own advice and googling it, I found that there is an open driver for unichrome gpu's.  I will look further to see if anyone has got this working with dual monitors.

you may want to check out openchrome.org

but since you are probably a noob you will need help compiling and installing the driver unless there is a deb which i doubt.


also have a look at this thread [http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=270667&page=1](http://ubuntuforums.org/showthread.php?t=270667&page=1)

### Post by MacAnthony on 2008-06-30
This is mostly directed to those helping with this issue: Is there a reason to not use xrandr?

I'm not sure if there are limitations to it, but that's what I use and it's way easier to use especially for laptops.

---

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-06-30
> **MacAnthony said:**
> This is mostly directed to those helping with this issue: Is there a reason to not use xrandr?

I'm not sure if there are limitations to it, but that's what I use and it's way easier to use especially for laptops.

The thing is he is using **via onboard graphics**. I personally don't think it will support dual monitors even with the openchrome drivers and xrandr.

It would be great if someone proved me wrong though :)

### Post by ThomasWii on 2008-06-30
ok, i have installed openChrome using manual install form [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/OpenChrome](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/OpenChrome) and changed my xorg from vesa to openchrome, now, if i run dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg or System > Preferences > Screen Res it dose not let me pick the driver. so is there a way of setting up a 2nd screen in the xorg.conf.

EDIT: this may be a sign, before i installed openchrome when i pressed Ctrl + Alt + L to lock the screen it went instally black, but now it fades out to black O.o

Thomas

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-06-30
> **ThomasWii said:**
> ok, i have installed openChrome using manual install form [https://help.ubuntu.com/community/OpenChrome](https://help.ubuntu.com/community/OpenChrome) and changed my xorg from vesa to openchrome, now, if i run dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg or System > Preferences > Screen Res it dose not let me pick the driver. so is there a way of setting up a 2nd screen in the xorg.conf.

EDIT: this may be a sign, before i installed openchrome when i pressed Ctrl + Alt + L to lock the screen it went instally black, but now it fades out to black O.o

EDIT2: also my screen saver has sped up O.o

Thanks
Thomas

There is a gui app to use now instead of dpkg-reconfigure xserver-xorg as of 8.04.

In a terminal run:

```
gksu displayconfig-gtk
```
[U]
Be careful as you could undo all your hard work.[/U]

Darn its a bug in displayconfig-gtk that seems to only happen with a multi monitor setup:

[https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/displayconfig-gtk/+bug/144410](https://bugs.launchpad.net/ubuntu/+source/displayconfig-gtk/+bug/144410)

Someone in that link had some sucess by editing out some lines in /etc/X11/xorg.conf it is described fully in the link.

---

### Post by ThomasWii on 2008-07-01
ok, > 

Same here. By removing these lines from a Device section of my xorg.conf I was able to launch displayconfig-gtk:

Option "TwinView" "true"
Option "TwinViewOrientation" "Clone"
Option "TVOutFormat" "COMPOSITE"
Option "TVStandard" "NTSC-M"
Option "SecondMonitorHorizSync" "30-50"
Option "SecondMonitorVertRefresh" "60"

Of course these lines may just be enabling the feature that causes it to crash, and the real problem is happening deeper down.

thats what they said, i dont have any of those lines.
Here's my xorg.conf ```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
	Identifier	"Generic Video Card"
	Driver		"openchrome"
	BusID		"PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
	Identifier	"Generic Monitor"
	Option		"DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-40
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
	Identifier	"Default Screen"
	Device		"Generic Video Card"
	Monitor		"Generic Monitor"
	DefaultDepth	24
	SubSection "Display"
		Modes		"1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600"
	EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		"Default Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
```

EDIT: if any one has a 2nd screen working, please post your xorg and i may be able to use the same section or whatever.

Thanks
Thomas

### Post by ThomasWii on 2008-07-01
ok, i'v edited my xorg acording to [http://ozlabs.org/~jk/docs/mergefb/]("http://ozlabs.org/~jk/docs/mergefb/") and my laptop screen still works fine, but not the external screen, heres the xorg, you will see that it has changed quite a bit.
```
# xorg.conf (X.Org X Window System server configuration file)
#
# This file was generated by dexconf, the Debian X Configuration tool, using
# values from the debconf database.
#
# Edit this file with caution, and see the xorg.conf manual page.
# (Type "man xorg.conf" at the shell prompt.)
#
# This file is automatically updated on xserver-xorg package upgrades *only*
# if it has not been modified since the last upgrade of the xserver-xorg
# package.
#
# If you have edited this file but would like it to be automatically updated
# again, run the following command:
#   sudo dpkg-reconfigure -phigh xserver-xorg

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Generic Keyboard"
	Driver		"kbd"
	Option		"XkbRules"	"xorg"
	Option		"XkbModel"	"pc105"
	Option		"XkbLayout"	"gb"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Configured Mouse"
	Driver		"mouse"
	Option		"CorePointer"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Identifier	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	Driver		"synaptics"
	Option		"SendCoreEvents"	"true"
	Option		"Device"		"/dev/psaux"
	Option		"Protocol"		"auto-dev"
	Option		"HorizEdgeScroll"	"0"
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"stylus"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"stylus"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"eraser"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"eraser"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "InputDevice"
	Driver		"wacom"
	Identifier	"cursor"
	Option		"Device"	"/dev/input/wacom"
	Option		"Type"		"cursor"
	Option		"ForceDevice"	"ISDV4"		# Tablet PC ONLY
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Generic Video Card (Primary)"
        Driver          "openchrome"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
EndSection

Section "Device"
        Identifier      "Generic Video Card (Secondary)"
        Driver          "openchrome"
        BusID           "PCI:1:0:0"
        Screen          1
EndSection


Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "Laptop LCD"
        Option          "DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-40
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "External Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
	HorizSync	28-40
	VertRefresh	43-60
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "Internal Screen"
        Device          "Generic Video Card (Primary)"
        Monitor         "Laptop LCD"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Modes           "1280x800" "1024x768" "800x600"
        EndSubSection
EndSection

Section "Screen"
        Identifier      "External Screen"
        Device          "Generic Video Card (Secondary)"
        Monitor         "External Monitor"
        DefaultDepth    24
        SubSection "Display"
                Modes           "1024x768"
        EndSubSection
EndSection


Section "ServerLayout"
	Identifier	"Default Layout"
	Screen		0 "Internal Screen"
	InputDevice	"Synaptics Touchpad"
	InputDevice     "stylus"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "cursor"	"SendCoreEvents"
	InputDevice     "eraser"	"SendCoreEvents"
EndSection
```
Thanks
Thomas

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-07-01
> Section "Monitor"
        Identifier      "External Monitor"
        Option          "DPMS"
[COLOR="Red"]	HorizSync	28-40
	VertRefresh	43-60[/COLOR]

You probably need to find the correct H and V refresh rates (marked in red) for your _specific model_ of monitor as they wont be the same as your laptop screen.

Google your monitors model number with the keyword _specs_ or post your monitor model number so i can have a look ;)

---

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-07-01
The external monitor section should go like this:

```
Section "Monitor"
Identifier "External Monitor"
Option "DPMS"
HorizSync 30-70
VertRefresh 50-160
```

After you edit and save xorg.conf, reboot and _hopefully_ your monitor will work ;)

50/50 though :(

---

### Post by ThomasWii on 2008-07-01
:( still dose not work, i wonder if ubuntu know its there or not, cos xrandr gives only my laptop screen :(

EDIT: is the "ServerLayout" section right?

Thomas

---

### Post by Tomatz on 2008-07-01
> **ThomasWii said:**
> :( still dose not work, i wonder if ubuntu know its there or not, cos xrandr gives only my laptop screen :(

EDIT: is the "ServerLayout" section right?

Thomas

The server layout section is fine.


The problem is that VIA do not officially support the gpu you have under Linux this is why you had to install the open source drivers (which are not made by VIA). 

This is a big problem in Linux at the moment (company's not supporting the OS) so people just use Nvidia or ATI cards which are officially supported by the manufacturers with regular driver updates.


If you want your dual monitor setup under Linux you will have to buy an Nvidia card unfortunatly (<ctrl> <alt> Right/Left Arrow switches workspaces BTW) :(

I'm just sorry i couldn't help more but saying that _YOU_ did most of the hard work yourself (without moaning and asking dumb questions) and you said you are only a kid so my hat goes off to you ;)


Welcome to Linux.

:popcorn:
